full_prompt: Design the chassis and body of an ultra-low fuel-consumption single-seat prototype vehicle for a fuel-economy race benchmark. The vehicle must use a supplied single-cylinder 4-stroke 50 cc engine (reference production 50 cc unit as permitted by the rulebook). The minimum turning radius of the finished vehicle must be no greater than 6 m, verified on a flat test pad. The overall body dimensions must comply with the rulebook envelope: vehicle length approximately 2.0-3.5 m, width approximately 0.5-1.3 m, height sufficient to safely house a seated driver (driver height reference 170 cm). The vehicle must have three or more wheels and positive stability (it must not tip over on a 25-degree static tilt test in any direction with the driver seated). The driver must be secured by a 4-point harness and be able to exit the vehicle unaided within 10 seconds. The primary structural chassis (whether tubular space frame, CFRP monocoque, or hybrid) must carry a quasi-static 80 kg driver mass applied to the seat pan with a 2.5 g vertical gust factor (equivalent 196 kg vertical load at the seat pan) and a 1.5 g lateral cornering load at the seat with no permanent yield. A front impact equivalent quasi-static deceleration of 5 g applied to a 180 kg (vehicle + driver) system must not produce yielding in the chassis primary structure or intrude into the driver cell. Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for the chassis under (a) seated driver + 2.5 g vertical, (b) 1.5 g lateral cornering, (c) 5 g front impact equivalent. A tilt analysis computes the maximum stable tilt angle from CG and track geometry — this is kinematic, not FEA. Vibration, thermal, fluid (CFD aerodynamic drag is highly relevant to mpg but is not in the rule-compliance gate), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required for the structural pass/fail gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the chassis + body, a mass budget, and a report listing turning radius in m, vehicle dry mass in kg, max von Mises stress under each load case in MPa, and tilt-to-tip angle in deg.
short_prompt:
Design the chassis and body of a single-seat prototype fuel-economy race vehicle around a supplied 50 cc 4-stroke engine. Overall envelope: length 2.0-3.5 m, width 0.5-1.3 m; driver reference 170 cm seated. Must have at least three wheels, be stable on a 25-degree static tilt in any direction with the driver seated, provide a 4-point harness, and permit unassisted egress within 10 s. Minimum turning radius 6 m on a flat test pad.

Load cases on the chassis (quasi-static equivalent).
- LC1 seated driver + 2.5 g vertical: 80 kg x 2.5 g at seat pan (~1,961 N).
- LC2 cornering 1.5 g lateral: 80 kg x 1.5 g at seat (~1,177 N).
- LC3 front-impact 5 g equivalent: 180 kg system x 5 g forward (~8,829 N) with no driver-cell intrusion.
- LC4 tilt test: 25 degrees static in any direction, no tip-over.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Tilt stability computed from CG height and track, not FEA.

prompt.title: Single-seat fuel-economy prototype chassis
prompt.brief: Design a 3-wheel ultra-low-drag fuel-economy vehicle around a 50 cc 4-stroke engine, min turning radius 6 m, 25 deg tilt stable.

material.typical: CFRP monocoque or 4130 steel tubular frame

load_cases.name: driver_vertical_2p5g
load_cases.description: 80 kg driver x 2.5 g on seat pan

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Safety factor 1.5 against yield in primary chassis
Ultimate SF 1.0 for 5 g crash equivalent, no driver cell intrusion

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell (monocoque) or beam (tube frame) + solid at joints

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Engine thermal is managed by OEM unit.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: CFD improves mpg but is not a pass/fail gate.
